origin.name: Finland
origin.death_registration: Digital and Population Data Services Agency (DVV)
origin.source: FCDO Finland travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Helsinki guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Finland is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is often simpler than full body repatriation. You will need the Kuolintodistus, cremation certificate, and urn transport documentation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Certified English translation of death certificate
special_rules: Finland is an EU member and Hague Apostille Convention member, which simplifies document legalisation.
Death registration is managed by the Digital and Population Data Services Agency (DVV).
The Prosecutor's Office orders post-mortem for suspicious or violent deaths.
All Finnish or Swedish-language documentation requires certified English translation for UK acceptance.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Finnish documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Helsinki can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Finland to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Finland. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-2 weeks. DVV registration, apostille, and documentation all handled. Contact us 24/7.
